app.subtitle: DESKTOP
health.title: System health
health.sub: Latency setting audit and desired-state drift vs live registry
health.hint: Safe = low risk only. Fix all = Safe + Moderate. Administrator rights required.
health.tip.refresh: Re-scan audit findings and drift vs desired-state
health.tip.fixSafe: Apply only Safe catalog tweaks for CanFix findings
health.tip.fixAll: Apply Safe and Moderate catalog tweaks for CanFix findings
health.tip.reapply: Rewrite registry values that drifted from desired-state
audit.hags.title: HAGS (hardware GPU scheduling)
audit.network_throttling.title: Network throttling index
audit.interrupt_steering.title: Interrupt steering
audit.serialize_timer.title: Serialize timer expiration
audit.power_throttling.title: Power throttling
audit.win32_priority.title: Win32 priority separation
audit.mouse_queue.title: Mouse data queue size
audit.keyboard_queue.title: Keyboard data queue size
audit.tcp_ack.title: TCP ACK frequency
audit.tcp_nodelay.title: TCP no-delay
audit.active_state.title: Optimization active state
settings.title: Settings
settings.sub: Tray, autostart, updates, and Windows logon behavior
settings.updates.desc: Check GitHub Releases. Installs from Program Files are silent (no folder wizard).
update.confirm: The app will close and install the update silently. Continue?
update.exiting: Setup started — the app will exit
update.portableHint: Portable build: use Setup from Releases for auto-update next time
update.err.network: Could not reach GitHub. Check network or open Releases manually.
update.err.timeout: Update check timed out. Try again or open Releases.
update.err.http: GitHub returned an error. Open Releases manually.
update.err.unknown: Update check failed. Open Releases for the latest Setup.
settings.tray.desc: Closing the window keeps the process running in the notification area.
settings.autostart.desc: Task Scheduler (on logon, highest privileges) — no UAC every boot after setup.
settings.autoapply.desc: Only after you enable once. Re-applies on next launch only if you left optimization ON (not after Reset / Disable).
settings.lifecycle.hint: On first Enable: tray + auto-optimize; Windows autostart only after a separate confirm; reboot is optional.
settings.tray.hint: Double-click the icon to open. Menu: optimize, reset, exit.
restart.title: Restart PC?
restart.body: Optimization is on. Timer and power plan are already active. A reboot is recommended so some registry/service tweaks fully apply — not required immediately.
restart.policies: Also enabled: minimize to tray and auto-optimize on later startups.
restart.skipped: You can restart later — some settings already work
empty.plugins.title: No plugins found
empty.plugins.desc: Modules appear after the engine starts. External plugins are *.plugin.dll files in the plugins folder.
empty.logs.title: No events yet
empty.logs.desc: Apply / Reset actions and plugin messages will show up here. Administrator rights are required for optimizations.
dash.title: System overview
dash.sub: Proxy scheduling latency (not DPC / not network) and optimization status
chart.title: Latency monitor
chart.offHint: Chart is off. Enable the switch to resume sampling.
chart.noteOn: Proxy scheduling latency (µs) — not kernel DPC or network · probe 15 ms · UI 50 ms · peak = max of last 60 s (glitches clamped)
chart.scale.tip: Click the left scale to lock Y: Auto → 200 → 500 → 1000 → 2000 → 5000 µs
hint.active: Click to restore the previous system state
tweaks.title: Plugins
tweaks.sub: Optimization modules. Experimental options remain off by default and are not included in Quick Boost.
power.title: Power management
power.sub: Select a profile, then use the main enable button on Overview (Quick Boost).
power.office.desc: Timer 1.0 ms, quieter hybrid cores, less heat.
power.max.desc: Ultimate Performance when available. Max heat.
power.gaming.desc: Timer 0.5 ms, High Performance, Game Mode / HAGS.
logs.title: Logs
logs.sub: Local activity · Administrator rights are required to apply optimizations
logs.footer: MIT · Win10 20H2+ / Win11 x64 · WebView2 + .NET 8 · Use Reset all if something feels wrong.
bar.disclaimer: No warranty · Use at your own risk
tip.unit.us: µs (microsecond) is one millionth of a second; lower values mean less latency.
tip.dpc: DPC is a deferred Windows kernel callback that can affect system responsiveness.
tip.hags: HAGS is hardware-accelerated GPU scheduling; it can reduce frame latency.
tip.timer.resolution: Timer Resolution is system timer precision; it affects thread scheduling.
plugin.stub.desc: MVP stub — toggle disabled, system is not changed
onboard.title: Before you enable
onboard.1: What changes: timer resolution, power plan, some GPU/Game Mode registry — with JSON backup.
onboard.2: Rollback: Reset all / Disable restores the saved state.
onboard.3: µs chart: proxy scheduling latency, not kernel DPC or network ping.
autostart.title: Start with Windows?
autostart.body: A Task Scheduler job (ONLOGON, highest) will be created so AntiLag Next can start elevated without UAC every logon.
autostart.note: You can turn this off in Settings. No task is created without confirmation.
plugin.core.timer.desc: NtSetTimerResolution hold — high impact on scheduling jitter. Managed by profile.
plugin.core.power.desc: High Performance / Ultimate, min CPU, ASPM off, core parking. Managed by profile.
plugin.core.gpu.desc: Registry low latency / pre-rendered frames (NVIDIA / AMD). May need reboot.
plugin.ext.network.qos.desc: Best-effort MMCSS Games task priorities. Not CoDel/UDP. Opt-in.
plugin.ext.registry.tweaks.desc: Network Throttling Index, GameDVR policy and related keys. Backed up to JSON.
plugin.ext.process.priority.desc: High priority for AntiLag Next and (when possible) games from the profile list.
plugin.ext.services.safe.desc: Set selected non-critical services to Manual (SysMain, DiagTrack, …). Opt-in.
plugin.exp.msi.desc: Experimental: Message Signaled Interrupts for devices. Stub — no automatic device changes.
plugin.exp.irq.affinity.desc: Experimental: IRQ affinity to cores. Not auto-applied (unsafe).
plugin.exp.driver.blacklist.desc: Experimental: driver list for manual mode. MVP never mutates boot-critical services.
wizard.title: Welcome to AntiLag Next
wizard.s1.body: AntiLag Next tunes timer resolution, power plan, and a curated set of latency registry keys to improve scheduling responsiveness.
wizard.s1.li2: Every change is backed up to local JSON — Reset all restores it.
wizard.s1.li3: Open-source · no inject into games · no telemetry.
wizard.s2.li1: Not a network ping booster or FPS magic multiplier.
wizard.s2.li2: Not NVIDIA Reflex / anti-cheat bypass / game inject.
wizard.s2.li3: µs chart = scheduling latency proxy — not kernel DPC truth.
wizard.s3.body: Ready when you are. Pick Gaming (recommended), then press Enable on the dashboard.
wizard.s3.li1: Health page: scan drift after Windows updates.
wizard.s3.li2: Export diagnostics from Logs/Settings if you need support data.
wizard.s3.li3: Something feels wrong? Reset all anytime.
ba.title: Before / After
ba.sub: Median µs over a sample window (~3 s / ≥12 points) before Enable and after settle
ba.hint: Enable optimization with the chart running to compare windows.
changed.title: What changed
changed.sub: Applied with JSON backup — Reset all restores prior state.
health.tip.fixRec: Apply Safe catalog fixes and reapply drifted desired-state
diag.title: Diagnostics
diag.desc: Local zip with redacted settings, audit, drift, and logs — no cloud upload.
audit.hags.detail: HAGS (HwSchMode) is off or missing — hardware GPU scheduling may help frame pacing.
audit.power_throttling.detail: PowerThrottlingOff is not set — Windows may throttle aggressively.
audit.win32_priority.detail: Win32PrioritySeparation differs from the recommended gaming value.
audit.mouse_queue.detail: Mouse data queue size is not optimal for low latency.
audit.keyboard_queue.detail: Keyboard data queue size is not optimal for low latency.
audit.tcp_ack.detail: TcpAckFrequency is not tuned for lower ACK delay.
audit.tcp_nodelay.detail: TCPNoDelay is not enabled (Nagle may add latency).
